title: Cephalonoplos setosus
intro: Cephalonoplos setosus, also known as Senna setosa, is a plant species in the Fabaceae family, commonly used as a medicinal herb. The health benefits of Cephalonoplos setosus include its use as a laxative, due to its high anthraquinone content, which can help to treat constipation and other gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include sennosides, anthraquinone glycosides, and other flavonoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Cephalonoplos setosus is typically used in herbal preparations such as tea, capsules, and extracts, often in combination with other herbs to promote digestive health and relieve constipation.
taxonomy: Cephalonoplos setosus is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It belongs to the genus Cephalonoplos. The genus Cephalonoplos is a monotypic genus, consisting of only one species, Cephalonoplos setosus. This species is native to Africa and is a type of legume. It is also known as the hairy stickpea due to its unique hairy appearance.
